title: molinia caerulea var. sylvatica
intro: Molinia caerulea var. sylvatica, also known as purple moor-grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its purple-red inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The health benefits of purple moor-grass include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Molinia caerulea var. sylvatica is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in essential oil form, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
